text: I feel a quiet curiosity in this space, standing before the glass doors leading into a vibrant, geometrically adorned room marked R+D. The play of fluorescent light over orderly displays and bold, thoughtful text sets my mind in motion. Here, the world appears calculated yet expressive, promising ideas and invention. There is a sense of anticipation, a slight unease beneath the surface, as if the walls themselves are inviting me to reflect on what it means to observe and to be observed. I feel both apart and implicated in this ongoing experiment of presence and perception.
